title: Responds to --help with help on stdout
rationale: --help is the first surface users and agents reach for. It must print help to stdout and exit 0.
remediation: Implement --help to print full usage to stdout and exit 0.

title: Responds to -h as a help alias
rationale: -h is the conventional short form of --help; supporting it meets user expectations.
remediation: Map -h to the same behavior as --help.

title: Help includes a usage synopsis
rationale: A usage/synopsis line shows the command shape at a glance before the option list.
remediation: Include a 'Usage:' (or 'Synopsis:') line in help output.

title: Help shows examples
rationale: Concrete examples are the fastest way for a human or agent to learn a command; clig.dev recommends leading with them.
remediation: Add an 'Examples:' section to help output with a few real invocations.
